_purpose: Topology types used in supply_origin.voltage_arrangement field + drawing_notes guidance. Defines the physical distribution strategy (single feeder, looped supply, split-phase, etc.).

distribution_types.label: Radial
distribution_types.description: Each board fed from one upstream feeder. No alternative path. Simplest topology; single point of failure per feeder.
distribution_types.selectivity_concern: Each stage must be selectively coordinated; partial selectivity acceptable if breaker ratings constrain room

distribution_types.label: Ring
distribution_types.description: Boards connected in a closed loop; each board fed from two directions (upstream and downstream feeder). Rare in modern practice; legacy method in some UK installations.
distribution_types.selectivity_concern: Requires special switchgear to isolate loop sections; risk of back-feed if not carefully controlled
limitations: Requires complex RCD coordination to prevent unintended disconnection
standards: BS 1363 (UK ring main circuits for outlets, not applicable to distribution boards)

distribution_types.label: Split-Phase (US 120/240V)
distribution_types.description: US residential and small commercial convention. Single-phase supply with center tap (neutral) providing 120V to neutral or 240V phase-to-phase. Not a topology in the sense of ring/radial, but a voltage arrangement.
distribution_types.selectivity_concern: N/A (no tiered distribution; all circuits directly from service panel)

distribution_types.label: Three-Phase Plus Neutral (TPN)
distribution_types.description: 3-phase + N. Most common commercial/industrial supply globally. Voltage typically 400V 3-ph (50Hz EU/KE) or 480V 3-ph (60Hz US). TN-S, TN-C-S, or TT subtypes per earthing system.
distribution_types.selectivity_concern: Each stage must be selectively coordinated; critical in multi-floor/zoned buildings

distribution_types.label: Three-Phase Plus Neutral + Separate Earth (TPN+E)
distribution_types.description: TPN with separate Earth conductor at intake. TN-S subtype in the earthing system taxonomy. Distinct PE from N throughout (not combined as TN-C-S).
distribution_types.selectivity_concern: Same as TPN; separate E allows independent PE sizing per BS 7430
typical_use_cases: Industrial estates in East Africa where separate E was installed for safety margin
Retrofit earthing upgrades (converting TN-C-S to TN-S)
standards: BS 7671:2018 Reg 411 (TN-S earthing system; allows separate PE)
